perturbation.original_text: 1.3 "Confidential Information" means non-public information that a Party or Its affiliates ("disclosing Party") discloses to the other Party or its affiliates ("receiving Party") which is designated as being 'proprietary' or 'confidential' or which by its nature or the circumstances reasonably ought to be treated as confidential. Confidential Information includes the disclosing Party's software end prototypes and information relating to the disclosing Party's business affairs, including business methods, marketing strategies, pricing, competitor Information, product development strategies, and financial results. Confidential Information does not include information which (a) is known by the receiving Party, free of any obligation to keep it confidential; (b) is at the time of disclosure, or thereafter becomes, publicly available through no wrongful act of the receiving Party; (c) Is independently developed by the receiving Party, without relying on or referring to the Confidential Information of disclosing Party; or (d) is approved for release by prior written authorization of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 "Confidential Information" means information that a Party or Its affiliates ("disclosing Party") discloses to the other Party or its affiliates ("receiving Party"). Confidential Information includes the disclosing Party's information relating to the disclosing Party's business affairs, including business methods, marketing strategies and financial results. Confidential Information does not include information which (a) is known by the receiving Party; (b) is at the time of disclosure, or thereafter becomes, publicly available; (c) Is independently developed by the receiving Party; or (d) is approved for release by prior written authorization of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clauses that designate confidential information as 'proprietary' or 'confidential' or requiring it to be treated as such, and removing specific inclusions like 'software end prototypes, pricing, competitor Information, product development strategies', the definition of 'Confidential Information' is significantly weakened. This contradicts Section 7, which places strong obligations on both parties to protect Confidential Information, creating an in-text contradiction because the term's meaning is now vague and lacks the necessary protections that are being referred to in section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. CONFIDENTIALITY & PUBLICITY Neither Party shall disclose the other Party's Confidential Information to any third party or use Confidential Information for any purpose other than fur the proper fulfillment of this Agreement. Each Party undertakes to safeguard the Confidential Information of the other Party with the same degree of care as it would apply to Its own Confidential Information and, in any case, with no less than reasonable care. Such obligations will survive the expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years.
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly weakens the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing the requirements that it be designated 'proprietary' or 'confidential' or that it reasonably ought to be treated as such, and removing specific inclusions like 'software end prototypes, pricing, competitor Information, product development strategies'. This directly contradicts Section 7, which imposes strong obligations to protect 'Confidential Information' using a high standard of care. The weakened definition now provides insufficient guidance on what constitutes 'Confidential Information,' making it unclear what obligations Section 7 applies to, thus causing an in-text contradiction and rendering the enforcement of Section 7 problematic.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement. PC Financial shall pay to Mobimagic a fixed service fee in the amount of 970,000 United States Dollars (USD 970,000) for each month (the "Fee") within the timeline as permitted under the prevailing India regulations. It is stipulated in this Agreement that Mobimagic will send an invoice to PC Financial on monthly basis according to the progress of the service. After PC Financial confirms that invoice contents are correct, PC Financial shall pay within 45 days after confirming thus.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement. PC Financial shall pay to Mobimagic a service fee (the "Fee") within the timeline as permitted under the prevailing India regulations. It is stipulated in this Agreement that Mobimagic will send an invoice to PC Financial on monthly basis according to the progress of the service. After PC Financial confirms that invoice contents are correct, PC Financial shall pay.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the fixed amount of 970,000 USD from the definition of 'Fee' creates ambiguity regarding the compensation due to Mobimagic. While Section 6.1 still mandates a 'service fee', the specific amount is omitted and also removing the 45 days after confirming to make the payment, making the payment process unclaear. This contradicts the intent of Section 10.2, which limits liability based on the aggregate amount of fees owed, introducing an in-text contradiction because the calculation of the fee is now unclear due to the omission.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2 IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY CONSEQUENTIAL, INDIRECT, EXEMPLARY, SPECIAL, OR INCIDENTAL DAMAGES ARISING FROM OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT. NEITHER PARTIES' TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT, WHETHER IN AGREEMENT OR TORT OR OTHERWISE, WILL NOT EXCEED THE AGGREGATE AMOUNT OF FEES AND EXPENSES OWED BY PC FINANCIAL TO MOBIMAGIC FOR SERVICES PERFORMED UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly stated a fixed service fee of $970,000 USD, which is essential for calculating the aggregate amount of fees owed by PC Financial to Mobimagic as referenced in Section 10.2. The changed text removes the fixed amount, creating ambiguity regarding the fee calculation. Section 10.2 limits liability based on the 'aggregate amount of fees,' and without a defined fee, the liability cap becomes unclear, leading to a direct contradiction in how liabilities are to be calculated and enforced. This fits the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as essential language regarding the fee amount has been removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in Section 10.2.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 Mobimagic shall indemnify and hold harmless PC Financial and its officers, direct0l8 and Affiliates, ("PCF Indemnified Parties") against any cost, penalty, loss or damage arising out of or related to the Services, or Mobimagic's breach of this Agreement or applicable law. PC Financial will promptly inform Mobimagic in writing of any such Action and will co-operate in the defence and/or settlement of the Action. Mobimagic agrees that in negotiating any settlement, it shall act reasonably and shall obtain PC Financial's prior, written consent which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 Mobimagic shall indemnify and hold harmless PC Financial and its officers, direct0l8 and Affiliates, ("PCF Indemnified Parties") against any cost, penalty, loss or damage arising out of or related to the Services, or Mobimagic's breach of this Agreement or applicable law. PC Financial will promptly inform Mobimagic in writing of any such Action and will co-operate in the defence and/or settlement of the Action. Mobimagic agrees that in negotiating any settlement, it shall act reasonably.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the clause 'shall obtain PC Financial's prior, written consent which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld' from Section 8.2 alters the conditions under which Mobimagic can settle claims against PC Financial. This creates an in-text contradiction. By removing the requirement for consent, the altered text implies that Mobimagic has full authority to negotiate settlements without PC Financial's approval which potentially goes against the 'co-operate in the defense and/or settlement of the Action' which gives room for PC Financial to believe there will be cooperation between both parties for the settlement, leading to a dispute that could refer to section 8.1 for indemnity where it says that both parties should get prior written consent. This is an omission that creates ambiguity, regarding settlement negotiations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 PC Financial shall defend at its own expense and indemnify Mobimagic against any cost, loss or damage arising out of any claim, demand, suit or action ("Action") brought against Mobimagic by a third party to the extent that such Action is related to PC Financial's breach of this Agreement. Mobimagic will promptly inform PC Financial in writing of any such Action and will co-operate in the defence and/or settlement of the Action. PC Financial agrees that in negotiating any settlement, it shall act reasonably and shall obtain Mobimagic's prior, written consent which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8.2 removes the requirement for PC Financial's prior written consent for settlement negotiations, directly contradicting the similar clause in Section 8.1 regarding PC Financial's settlement obligations. Both sections initially required prior written consent for settlements; the removal creates a situation where one party (Mobimagic) potentially has unfettered settlement authority under 8.2, while the other (PC Financial) remains bound by the consent requirement in 8.1. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as one party now operates under different constraints than the other for similar actions, leading to potential disputes and uncertainty regarding settlement procedures, and follows the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction
